system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "system";
    object      controlDict;
}

application     rhoCentralFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         0.2;

deltaT          0.0001;

writeControl    adjustableRunTime;

writeInterval   0.02;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable yes;

adjustTimeStep  no;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

fluxScheme      Kurganov;

ddtSchemes
{
    default         Euler;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(tauMC)      Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
    reconstruct(rho) vanLeer;
    reconstruct(U)  vanLeerV;
    reconstruct(T)  vanLeer;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    "(rho|rhoU|rhoE)"
    {
        solver          diagonal;
    }

    U
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        nSweeps         2;
        tolerance       1e-09;
        relTol          0.01;
    }

    e
    {
        $U;
        tolerance       1e-09;
        relTol          0.01;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='momentumTransport', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "constant";
    object      momentumTransport;
}

simulationType  laminar;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='physicalProperties', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "constant";
    object      physicalProperties;
}

thermoType
{
    type            hePsiThermo;
    mixture         pureMixture;
    transport       const;
    thermo          hConst;
    equationOfState perfectGas;
    specie          specie;
    energy          sensibleInternalEnergy;
}

mixture
{
    specie
    {
        molWeight   11640.3;
    }
    thermodynamics
    {
        Cp          2.5;
        Hf          0;
    }
    transport
    {
        mu          0;
        Pr          1;
    }
}'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='U', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile
{

    format      ascii;

internalField   uniform (10 0 0);

boundaryField

    }

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='p', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile

boundaryField

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='blockMeshDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

vertices

blocks
(

edges
(

boundary

        faces
        (
            (0 3 2 1)
            (4 5 6 7)
        );
    }
);

mergePatchPairs
(
);

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='T', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile

<reviewer_analysis>## Error Analysis

### 1. `log.blockMesh` — Face Topology Conflict (Same Issue as Attempt 1)

**Error:**  
```
Trying to specify a boundary face 4(3 7 6 2) on the face on cell 0 which is either an internal face or already belongs to some other patch.
```

This is the **exact same error** as in Attempt 1. The problem hasn't been resolved yet. Looking more carefully at the current `blockMeshDict`:

The `top` patch contains face `(3 7 6 2)`. Let me trace the vertices:
- Vertex 3 = `(-0.15242, 0.09144, -0.005)`
- Vertex 7 = `(-0.15242, 0, 0.005)`
- Vertex 6 = `(-0.15242, 0.09144, 0.005)`
- Vertex 2 = `(0, 0.09144, -0.005)`

But wait — **vertex 2** `(0, 0.09144, -0.005)` is shared with **block 1**. This means face `(3 7 6 2)` spans across both blocks! That's impossible — each face must belong to exactly one block. Since vertex 2 is part of block 1, face `(3 7 6 2)` cannot be assigned to block 0 alone.

Similarly, looking at the `frontAndBack` patch, face `(0 3 2 1)` includes vertex 2, which again belongs to block 1. And face `(4 5 6 7)` includes vertex 6, which also belongs to block 1.

The fundamental issue is that **all four faces listed under `frontAndBack` span multiple blocks**, making them invalid boundary faces. Each face must consist of vertices that all belong to the SAME block.

#### Root Cause:
The vertex numbering creates ambiguity. Vertices 2, 6, 11, and 15 are shared between blocks, and several proposed boundary faces include vertices from both blocks, making those faces cross-block and therefore invalid.

#### Solution:
Redefine the vertex layout so that **each block has completely disjoint vertex sets** (no shared vertices). Then redefine all boundary faces using only vertices from their respective single block.

Here’s the corrected approach:

**Block 0 (lower region):** Keep vertices 0–7 as-is.  
**Block 1 (upper region):** Shift all vertices up by 0.09144 in Y so they don’t overlap with block 0. Change block 1 vertices to:
- 8 → `(0, 0.18288, -0.005)`  
- 9 → `(0.3048, 0.18288, -0.005)`  
- 10 → `(0.3048, 0.23432, -0.005)`  
- 11 → `(0, 0.23432, -0.005)`  
- 12 → `(0, 0.18288, 0.005)`  
- 13 → `(0.3048, 0.18288, 0.005)`  
- 14 → `(0.3048, 0.23432, 0.005)`  
- 15 → `(0, 0.23432, 0.005)`

Now each block has unique vertices. Update the boundary faces accordingly:

- `top`: Only face `(3 7 6 2)` — this is entirely within block 0 ✓
- `bottom`: Add face `(8 12 13 9)` — this is entirely within block 1 ✓
- `frontAndBack`: Only keep faces that are entirely within one block:
  - From block 0: `(0 3 2 1)` and `(4 5 6 7)`
  - From block 1: Add new faces like `(8 9 10 11)` and `(12 13 14 15)`

Alternatively, a simpler fix: **remove the `top` and `frontAndBack` patches entirely** since they involve faces that cross block boundaries. Instead, make the entire top/bottom/y-extents handled by the `symmetryPlane` and `empty` patches, and let the two blocks stack vertically without sharing any boundary faces. Just ensure all boundary faces refer to vertices belonging to a single block.

### 2. `log.rhoCentralFoam` — Dependent on successful blockMesh

Once blockMesh succeeds and generates valid `points`, `faces`, etc., `rhoCentralFoam` will run. No additional changes needed there beyond what was done previously.</reviewer_analysis>
<rewrite_plan>{'target_files': [{'file': 'system/blockMeshDict', 'changes': 'Change block 1 vertex 8 from (0 0.09144 -0.005) to (0 0.18288 -0.005); Change block 1 vertex 9 from (0.3048 0.09144 -0.005) to (0.3048 0.18288 -0.005); Change block 1 vertex 10 from (0.3048 0.1524 -0.005) to (0.3048 0.23432 -0.005); Change block 1 vertex 11 from (0 0.1524 -0.005) to (0 0.23432 -0.005); Change block 1 vertex 12 from (0 0.09144 0.005) to (0 0.18288 0.005); Change block 1 vertex 13 from (0.3048 0.09144 0.005) to (0.3048 0.18288 0.005); Change block 1 vertex 14 from (0.3048 0.1524 0.005) to (0.3048 0.23432 0.005); Change block 1 vertex 15 from (0 0.1524 0.005) to (0 0.23432 0.005); Remove top patch entry since it crosses block boundaries; Replace bottom patch faces list with only face (8 12 13 9) which is entirely within block 1; Move face (0 3 2 1) from frontAndBack to bottom patch; Move face (4 5 6 7) from frontAndBack to bottom patch; Add face (0 1 4 3) to bottom patch; Add face (8 9 10 11) to bottom patch; Add face (12 13 14 15) to bottom patch; Set bottom patch type to wall instead of symmetryPlane'}]}</rewrite_plan>

<user_requirement>Perform a 2D supersonic flow simulation over a wedge. Create a 3D geometry spanning from -0.15242 m to 0.3048 m in the x-direction, 0 m to 0.1524 m in the y-direction, and -0.005 m to 0.005 m in the z-direction. The simulation starts at 0.0 seconds and runs until 0.2 seconds with a time step (deltaT) of 0.0001 seconds. The results are written every 0.02 seconds. The inlet velocity is 10 m/s, inlet temperature is 1 K and inlet pressure is 1 Pa. The wedge boundary uses slip boundary condition for velocity. Use normalized thermodynamic properties where molWeight is set to 11640.3, Cp is 2.5, Pr is 1.0, and dynamic viscosity mu is 0. This corresponds to a gas constant R of 0.7143 and a specific heat ratio of 1.4. Use rhoCentralFoam solver.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
